lede: A look at March 18 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Brenden Rice and Cory Schneider, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: March 2025 Israeli attacks on the Gaza Strip
events.desc: Israel launches widespread aerial bombardments and attacks on the Gaza Strip, killing at least 591 people, including children.

events.title: Ozone Disco fire
events.desc: A nightclub fire in Quezon City, Philippines kills 162 people.

events.title: Lon Nol
events.desc: Lon Nol ousts Prince Norodom Sihanouk of Cambodia.

events.title: Mount Vesuvius
events.desc: Mount Vesuvius in Italy erupts, killing 26 people, causing thousands to flee their homes, and destroying dozens of Allied bombers.

events.title: 1925 Tri-State tornado
events.desc: The 1925 Tri-State tornado hits the Midwestern states of Missouri, Illinois, and Indiana, killing 695 people.

events.title: World War I
events.desc: World War I: During the Battle of Gallipoli, three battleships are sunk during a failed British and French naval attack on the Dardanelles.

events.title: George I of Greece
events.desc: King George I of Greece is assassinated in the recently liberated city of Thessaloniki.

events.title: American Revolution
events.desc: American Revolution: The British Parliament repeals the Stamp Act.

events.title: Susenyos I
events.desc: Susenyos is formally crowned Emperor of Ethiopia.

events.title: First Mongol invasion of Poland
events.desc: First Mongol invasion of Poland: Mongols overwhelm Polish armies in Kraków in the Battle of Chmielnik and plunder the city.

deaths.text: Harrison McCain, Canadian businessman, co-founded McCain Foods
Patrick Smith (politician), Irish farmer and politician, Minister for Agriculture, Food and the Marine
Anne Robert Jacques Turgot, French economist and politician, Controller-General of Finances

observances.text: Christian feast day: Blessed Aimée-Adèle Le Bouteiller
